title: Cell DIVE™ Platform | Ab Conjugation: Initial Conjugation & Scale up Conjugation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bsiob","text":"Scope","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bibkc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"set6","text":"The purpose of this protocol is to conjugate purified antibodies to Cy dyes. Antibody conjugation involves combining dyes with antibodies to achieve a fluorescently labeled antibody. Each antibody will be labeled with a molar equivalent of dye; the quantitative output of this is deemed D/P. In the initial conjugation step, each antibody will be labeled with differing D/P levels and validated to determine which level is optimal.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines:
{"blocks":[{"key":"b613s","text":"Associated Documents","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":20}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5ceor","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7prmi","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"dlmbl","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6gq7a","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cfmn","text":"Definitions & Descriptions","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":26}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2rdaq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7fkpb","text":"   Definitions","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1c5ol","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"232qp","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"egdcl","text":"Descriptions","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7vjpc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dbope","text":"In a given assay, when an antibody gets down selected from P/S validation proceed to Section 8 for initial conjugation. This section describes the process of conjugating an antibody at two D/P levels.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"13thh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"flrsa","text":"After the conjugate is made, a biologist will validate the two different D/P and select the best D/P based on specificity and sensitivity. After the D/P down-selection, the user proceeds to Section 9 for the process of scaling up the conjugate (if needed).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8ltc8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2rkt2","text":"If antibody has been conjugated previously and it is in one of our DC catalogues, please proceed immediately to Section 9 for the scale up conjugation protocol; you do not need to do the pilot conjugation for validated DCs.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f96vh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7m95u","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3pklr","text":"Troubleshooting","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7jknj","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"gf8t","text":" Re-conjugate the same conjugate to a desired D/P"],["If conjugation efficiency is >10% <40%","Dye calculation errors","Check spreadsheet for errors, if needed re-measure dye concentration"],["If purification yield is < 50%","Precipitation","Do conjugation at 500 μg/mL"],["If conjugate precipitates after purification","Concentration might be too high","Dilute the conjugate to 100 μg/mL and when conjugate is made next time – make at 500 μg/mL and read the conjugates after purification immediately and dilute them to 100 μg/mL"],["D/P ratios are out of spec

before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"9ov1j","text":"Critical Parameters","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7fgn8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6hk2u","text":"Please take particular note of the following instructions regarding critical steps:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":83},{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":83}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"de5nn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"efcoa","text":"It is essential to read the complete instruction booklet before starting work.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3q0t4","text":"These instructions have only been validated on formalin-fixed paraffin embedded tissue sections.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"139vl","text":"Unless noted, it is essential to allow reagents to reach room temperature prior to use.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"60gfo","text":"Mix samples and all reagents thoroughly before use.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7mrq","text":"Avoid extensive exposure of fluorescent reagents to ambient light.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"24k1s","text":"Safety warnings and precautions","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":31}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5o0lt","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"74rpu","text":"Warning: For research use only.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8kd1t","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7f0k7","text":"Cell DIVE software and workflows are for internal research use only and not for third party service use or clinical diagnosis. Do not use internally or externally in humans or animals. All chemicals should be considered as potentially hazardous. We therefore recommend that this product is handled only by those persons who have been trained in laboratory techniques and that it is used in accordance with the principles of good laboratory practice. Wear suitable protective clothing such as laboratory coats, safety glasses, and gloves. Care should be taken to avoid contact with skin or eyes. In the case of contact with skin or eyes wash immediately with water.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3ihnq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6h2vu","text":"For hazard information and safety warnings, please refer to the SDS (Safety Data Sheet).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

• Expiration Date: Do not use after date stamped on manufacturer’s label"]],"rowHeights":[23,127,127],"show_headers":true}},"4":{"type":"tables","mutability":"","data":{"cellsMeta":{"A_1":{"className":"_dt-bold"},"B_1":{"className":"_dt-bold"}},"colTitles":["",""],"colWidths":[219,"211.00000381469727"],"data":[["Equipment","Intended Use"],["NanoDrop 2000c","Used for measuring Absorbance of Proteins (280nm) and Dye’s (variable wavelength)"],["Microcentrifuge","Used for spinning antibodies down prior to conjugation and for pilot conjugation purification"],["Fisher Vortex Genie 2","Used for mixing of solutions"],["Denver Instruments UB-10","pH meter used to check pH of reagents"],["Analytical Balance","Used to weigh reagents"],["Fixed Speed Centrifuge","Used to spin down solutions after mixing via vortex"],["Laptop connected to NanoDrop","Drives NanoDrop software"]],"guid":"58FFE350317D11EB8FFFA5A0212E1601","isJexcelDataFormat":true,"legend":"","mergeCells":{},"printData":[["Equipment","Intended Use"],["NanoDrop 2000c","Used for measuring Absorbance of Proteins (280nm) and Dye’s (variable wavelength)"],["Microcentrifuge","Used for spinning antibodies down prior to conjugation and for pilot conjugation purification"],["Fisher Vortex Genie 2","Used for mixing of solutions"],["Denver Instruments UB-10","pH meter used to check pH of reagents"],["Analytical Balance","Used to weigh reagents"],["Fixed Speed Centrifuge","Used to spin down solutions after mixing via vortex"],["Laptop connected to NanoDrop","Drives NanoDrop software"]],"rowHeights":[23,23,23,23,23,23,23,23],"show_headers":true}},"5":{"type":"notes","mutability":"","data":{"blocks":[{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[{"length":6,"offset":0,"style":"italic"}],"key":"5lblf","text":"Note: Follow proper disposal for all reagents. Safety information and disposal is located in the laboratory. Be sure to label all reagents with appropriate name, date made, expiration date and safety label.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}},"6":{"type":"tables","mutability":"","data":{"blocks":[{"data":{},"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[{"length":6,"offset":0,"style":"italic"}],"key":"5lblf","text":"Note: Follow proper disposal for all reagents. Safety information and disposal is located in the laboratory. Be sure to label all reagents with appropriate name, date made, expiration date and safety label.","type":"unstyled"}],"cellsMeta":{"A_1":{"className":"_dt-bold"},"B_1":{"className":"_dt-bold"}},"colTitles":["",""],"colWidths":[226,"233.00000381469727"],"data":[["Reagents","Definitions"],["1X PBS","• Quantity: 1 L
• Composition: 100 mL 10X PBS, 900 mL dH2O
• Storage: Room Temperature.
• Expiration Date: One year from date of stock receipt.
• Mix thoroughly into a 1 L bottle."],["Cy Dye 1X Stock Solution","• Take a pinch from the 5 mg vial and dissolve in 40 μL DMSO
• Storage Conditions: 4°C, wrap vial in parafilm and store in aluminum storage sleeve
• Expiration: One week after creation
• Thaw by placing aluminum sleeve containing the vial on the bench top for 10 minutes at 21-27°C. Vortex and spin before using"],["Dye Concentration Measurement Solution","• Quantity: 500 μL
• Composition: 2 μL Cy Dye 1X Stock Solution, 498 μL 1X PBS
• Storage Conditions: Store at 21-27°C
• Expiration date: one hour from time and date made
• Prepare solution immediately before use, discard after one hour"],["Cy Dye 0.1X Working Solution","• Quantity: Volumes and Working Dilutions will be dependent on amount of antibody being conjugated
• Composition: 1 Part Cy Dye 1X Stock Solution, 9 Parts 1X PBS
• Storage conditions: Discard immediately after use
• Expiration: Discard immediately after use"],["Cy Dye 0.2X Working Solution","• Quantity: Volumes and Working Dilutions will be dependent on amount of antibody being conjugated
• Composition: 1 Part Cy Dye 1X Stock Solution, 4 Parts 1X PBS
• Storage conditions: Discard immediately after use
• Expiration: Discard immediately after use"],["Cy Dye 0.33X Working Solution","• Quantity: Volumes and Working Dilutions will be dependent on amount of antibody being conjugated
• Composition: 1 Part Cy Dye 1X Stock Solution, 2 Parts 1X PBS
• Storage conditions: Discard immediately after use
• Expiration: Discard immediately after use"],["Cy Dye 0.5X Working Solution","• Quantity: Volumes and Working Dilutions will be dependent on amount of antibody being conjugated
• Composition: 1 Part Cy Dye 1X Stock Solution, 1 Part 1X PBS
• Storage conditions: Discard immediately after use
• Expiration: Discard immediately after use"],["Stabilizing Solution (1% BSA/0.45% Sodium Azide)","• Quantity: 1000 μL
• Composition: 10 mg BSA, 693 uL 0.1M Sodium Azide; Dilute up to 1000 μL with 1X PBS
• Storage Conditions: 2-8°C
• Expiration: Two months from date of creation"]],"entityMap":{},"guid":"B7F6ACEF334011EBA5C20A58A9FEAC2A","isJexcelDataFormat":true,"legend":"","mergeCells":{},"printData":[["Reagents","Definitions"],["1X PBS","• Quantity: 1 L
• Composition: 100 mL 10X PBS, 900 mL dH2O
• Storage: Room Temperature.
• Expiration Date: One year from date of stock receipt.
• Mix thoroughly into a 1 L bottle."],["Cy Dye 1X Stock Solution","• Take a pinch from the 5 mg vial and dissolve in 40 μL DMSO
• Storage Conditions: 4°C, wrap vial in parafilm and store in aluminum storage sleeve
• Expiration: One week after creation
• Thaw by placing aluminum sleeve containing the vial on the bench top for 10 minutes at 21-27°C. Vortex and spin before using"],["Dye Concentration Measurement Solution","• Quantity: 500 μL
• Composition: 2 μL Cy Dye 1X Stock Solution, 498 μL 1X PBS
• Storage Conditions: Store at 21-27°C
• Expiration date: one hour from time and date made
• Prepare solution immediately before use, discard after one hour"],["Cy Dye 0.1X Working Solution","• Quantity: Volumes and Working Dilutions will be dependent on amount of antibody being conjugated
• Composition: 1 Part Cy Dye 1X Stock Solution, 9 Parts 1X PBS
• Storage conditions: Discard immediately after use
• Expiration: Discard immediately after use"],["Cy Dye 0.2X Working Solution","• Quantity: Volumes and Working Dilutions will be dependent on amount of antibody being conjugated
• Composition: 1 Part Cy Dye 1X Stock Solution, 4 Parts 1X PBS
• Storage conditions: Discard immediately after use
• Expiration: Discard immediately after use"],["Cy Dye 0.33X Working Solution","• Quantity: Volumes and Working Dilutions will be dependent on amount of antibody being conjugated

steps: Please select between the following cases:

Note: In case if your institution does not have NanoDrop, please use a UV spectrophotometer of your choice. For Cy2 - measure absorption at 489 nm. The Cy2 extinction coefficient is 150,000. Divide absorption at 550 nm by 0.15 to get concentration in μM. Molar Concentration (mM) = (Average μM concentration X 250 dilution factor)/1000. For Cy3 - measure absorption at 550 nm. The Cy3 extinction coefficient is 150,000. Divide absorption at 550nm by 0.15 to get concentration in μM. Molar Concentration (mM) = (Average μM concentration X 250 dilution factor)/1000. For Cy5 - measure absorption at 650 nm. The Cy5 extinction coefficient is 250,000. Divide absorption at 650 nm by 0.25 to get concentration in μM. Molar Concentration (mM) = (Average μM concentration X 250 dilution factor)/1000. For Cy7 - measure absorption at 750 nm. The Cy7 extinction coefficient is 200,000. Divide absorption at 650 nm by 0.2 to get concentration in μM. Molar Concentration (mM) = (Average μM concentration X 250 dilution factor)/1000.
From the home screen select “Proteins and Labels”.
Select IgG under “Type”, as per Figure 1.1. Figure 1.1 Selecting IgG as the protein Type

Select the dye to be measured from the dropdown menu for “Dye1,” as per (1) on Figure 1.2. NOTE 1: Cy2 is not a standard option in the NanoDrop software. You will need to add Cy2 to the dye list. Consult the instrument software manual for specific instructions. NOTE2: Cy7 is incompatible with the “Proteins and Labels” Program. Use the “UV-Vis” program to read Cy7 dye and conjugates. Figure 1.2: Setting Dye Parameters on NanoDrop
Measure 2 µL, fresh 1X PBS and place on NanoDrop pedestal to blank the instrument.
Select “Blank.”
After the measurement completes wipe the pedestal with a Kimwipe.
Measure 2 µL, fresh 1X PBS and place on NanoDrop pedestal to measure the baseline.
Select “Measure.”
After the measurement completes wipe the pedestal with a Kimwipe.

Refer to Figure 1.3: Ensuring a Stable Baseline on the NanoDrop. Figure 1.3: Ensuring a Stable Baseline on the NanoDrop A Stable Baseline should read an absorbance between 0.02 and –0.02, with no tilt in the horizontal line. An Unstable Baseline will look tilted or veer outside of the acceptable -0.02 and +0.02 acceptable range.

Measure 2 µL, of the dye concentration measurement solution (Section 'Materials' for preparation, in μM) 5 times, taking 5 independent readings. Spoiler - the 'dye concentration measurement solution' is 2µL of the Cy dye stock solution mixed with 498µL of PBS.
The instrument will calculate the dye concentration.
Refer to (2) of Figure 1.4: Setting Dye Parameters on NanoDrop. Figure 1.4: Measuring Cy7 on the NanoDrop
Cy7 measurements: The measurements for Cy7 dye and conjugates are slightly different.
From the home screen select “UV-Vis”.
Change the “Baseline correction” wavelength to a wavelength where the signal is expected to be 0. Refer to Figure 1.4, Step 1.
To calculate the molar concentration of the Cy dye 1X stock solution: Molar Concentration (mM) = (Average μM concentration X 250 dilution factor)/1000

Write the calculated concentration on the aluminum storage sleeve along with the date. This Molar Concentration (mM) will be used as the Dye Conc. in Form-1 and Form-7. Note - preferred but not necessary; Cy dye 1x stock solution concentration is preferred to be ~3 mM. If the concentration is over 3.5 mM, dilute with DMSO, if the concentration is below 2 mM, add more dye powder and return to step 2.
Add 280 and 750 nm to the list of wavelengths to be measured. Refer to Figure 1.4, Step 2.

In the UV-Vis program, the absorbance is displayed for a 1 mm path length and the dye concentration is not automatically calculated. The dye concentration for Cy7 is calculated as follows: Dye concentration (μM) = Absorbance 750nm *50

Refer to Table 2.1, for specific parameters for the antibody to be conjugated. %s %s NOTE: In the event antibody purification yield is low or the vendor supplies insufficient quantity to perform 2 D/P per dye, inform the study coordinator and, if approved, continue conjugating 1 D/P per dye.
The pilot conjugations will be used to determine the optimal D/P for the specific antibody tested. Every antibody will have an optimal D/P.

Determine the minimum quantity to conjugate. Be sure to save at least 5 µg, unconjugated primary antibody to be validated alongside the direct conjugate. Optimally, 45 µg reactions should be performed; however, a minimum of 30 µg is required for every conjugation, regardless of study. If the conjugation will be used beyond initial testing, i.e. for a multiplexing run, refer to Form-4: Amount of Antibody Required for Initial Direct Conjugation, to input the data and calculate the optimal amount of antibody to conjugate. Consult the study coordinator to determine the number of slides required for the study. (This form will calculate the minimum amount of antibody needed to conjugate based on the study. If purification is required, refer to the purification manual.)

Use Form-7 to calculate the volume of antibody, 20X borate, 1X PBS and dye needed for the pilot reaction Input any cell that is colored blue “Dye purity” is the percent (%) reactive dye information that will be on the Certificate of Analysis and is lot dependent. Enter the information on Form 7 in decimal form (i.e. 90.2% = 0.902). If the information is not available, use the following website to look up the CoA using the lot#: https://www.cytivalifesciences.com/en/us/support/quality/certificates “Loading” is the initial ratio of dye to antibody that is used in the conjugation. The loading is calculated as: Initial D/P divided by expected efficiency (expressed as a decimal). Refer to Table 2.1 for the appropriate values. For example, if targeting a Cy3 labeling at a D/P of 3.5, the “Loading” would be 4.67 (3.5/0.75). Choose a dye dilution that will allow you to add a minimum of 2 μL of solution while not overly diluting the entire sample. As you adjust the “dye dilution” cell on Form-7, the “μL dye” will change accordingly. This volume is the amount of the diluted dye that you need to add. See Section 'Materials' for the preparation of the working dye solutions. If the dilution needed is not described in Section 'Materials', adjust the calculations accordingly. You should prepare at least twice the volume of working solution that will need to be added to the conjugation. If there is enough antibody, repeat the calculations for the second D/P target. Refer to Figure 2.1, for an example of calculating these values. On Form-7, there are two choices of calculations depending on your starting antibody concentration. Figure 2.1: Antibody Conjugation Preparation

Remove the aluminum jacket containing the Cy Dye 1X Stock Solution from its storage location. If the dye is being removed from the refrigerator allow it to thaw at Room temperature for approximately 00:15:00 , while remaining in the aluminum jacket.
Combine volumes specified in go to step #23 for the antibody, fresh 1X PBS and borate into a 1.5 mL eppendorf tube using a pipette.
Vortex the mixture for 00:00:03 at a moderate speed to thoroughly mix.

Spin the mixture in the Fixed Speed Centrifuge for 00:00:05 . Do not allow the mixture to incubate for more than 5 minutes before adding the dye working dilution, as the antibody structure could be compromised.

Make the dye dilution calculated in go to step #23 carefully by adding the calculated amount of Cy Dye 1X Stock Solution to 1X PBS. To ensure that unwanted dye has not stuck to the outside of the pipette tip, gently wipe the outside of the pipette tip with a Kimwipe before diluting in 1X PBS. See image below:
Vortex the dye working solution for 00:00:03 at a speed setting of 9.
Spin the solution in the fixed speed centrifuge for 00:00:05 .
Add the appropriate amount of working Dye Solution, calculated in go to step #23 , within 1 minute to the antibody/borate mixture to prevent dye hydrolysis.
Place solution in an antibody cardboard box in a drawer containing no light.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:30:00 . Use a timer to ensure reaction does not go over 30 minutes .
Begin this process immediately after conjugate incubation has commenced (step 33).
Remove two Zeba Spin 0.5 mL columns from their packaging. If doing multiple conjugations simultaneously, prepare 2 columns per conjugation.
Remove and dispose of bottom closures and loosen caps.
Place columns into 1.5 mL eppendorf tubes.
Remove storage solution by spinning them into a microcentrifuge at 1500 x g, 23 °C, 1 minute .
The storage solutions for the columns will have passed through and the chromatography resin will now be compacted.
Discard the caps, storage buffers, and place a vertical mark where resin is slanted upwards. Figure 4.1: Vertical Mark On Resin Slant
Add 300 µL, fresh 1X PBS solution to columns.
Place the columns back in the microcentrifuge. Ensure the marked side is facing towards the outside of the centrifuge.
Centrifuge at 1500 x g, °C, 1 minute .
Transfer prepared columns to new, clean eppendorf tubes labeled with the antibody name.
If needed, add fresh 1X PBS to the conjugation reaction after the 30 minute incubation period is over so that the total volume of the conjugation reaction is > 70 µL .

Apply the conjugation reaction solution to the center of one of the Zeba spin columns. Be sure not to disturb the surface of the compacted resin. Refer to Figure 5.1. The minimum volume that can be loaded into a zeba column is 70 µL . Figure 5.1: Direct Conjugate Purification Workflow
After the sample has been put on the column measure 15 µL, fresh 1X PBS and apply to the top of the column. This is referred to as a stacker and will help in overall antibody yield.
Spin in the microcentrifuge at 1500 x g, 23 °C, 2 minutes . Be sure the black line on the column is facing outward.
The purified direct conjugate will flow through the column and collect in the bottom of the 1.5 mL eppendorf tube.
Remove the purified DC from the first column tube and place the sample onto the center of the second prepared column, be sure not to apply sample to the side of the compacted resin.
Spin in the microcentrifuge at 1500 x g, 23 °C, 2 minutes . This is to ensure complete removal of any unbound dye.
Discard the used Zeba columns and proceed to quantitative assessment.
Open the NanoDrop 2000c software version 7.2.1.1 program located on the desktop of the NanonDrop computer
From the home screen select “Proteins and Labels”. If Cy7 was used, follow alternative procedure described earlier ( go to step #17 ).
Select IgG under “Type”. (Refer to (1) on Figure 6.1) Figure 6.1: Quantitative Assessment of Direct Conjugates
Select dye under Dye 1: Refer to (1) of Figure 1.2 ( go to step #6 ).
Measure 2 µL, fresh 1X PBS and place on NanoDrop pedestal to blank the instrument.
Select “Blank.”
After the measurement completes wipe the pedestal with a Kimwipe.
Measure 2 µL, fresh 1X PBS and place on NanoDrop pedestal to measure the baseline.
Select “Measure.”
After the measurement completes wipe the pedestal with a Kimwipe.

Refer to Figure 1.3 ( go to step #13 ): Ensuring a Stable Baseline on the NanoDrop 2000c. If the baseline is poor, repeat go to step #58 A Stable Baseline should read and Absorbance between 0.02 and -0.02, with no tilt in the horizontal line. An Unstable Baseline will look tilted or veer outside of the acceptable -0.02 and +0.02 acceptable range.

Measure the purified direct conjugate 3 times , verify the A 280 and A Dye . Refer to Figure 6.1. (2) is the protein absorbance at 280nm. (3) is the dye absorbance at the dye specified wavelength. Figure 6.1: Quantitative Assessment of Direct Conjugates

Calculate the direct conjugate D/P and efficiency. Use the appropriate form based on the dye conjugated# Form-2:Cy3 Direct Conjugate Form-3:Cy5 Direct Conjugate Form-5: Cy2 Direct Conjugate Form-6: Cy7 Direct Conjugate Refer to Figure 8.6.2. Figure 6.2: Pilot Direct Conjugate Dye To Protein Calculation
Take a volumetric reading of the DC with a p200 pipette.
Input the volume of the DC into “Volume of DC (uL)” on the D/P calculation sheet to determine the yield of the direct conjugate.
Ideally, the two conjugates should be separated by at least 1.5 D/P. If they are not, a new conjugate should be made utilizing the pilot efficiency to obtain the acceptable D/P levels.

The acceptable range of D/P is listed on Table 2.1 ( go to step #20 ) for each dye. If the conjugates fall outside of this range consult the study coordinator to determine if the conjugate should be tested, or a new lot of conjugate should be made.
Refer to the appropriate D/P calculation form depending on what dye was used.

The template will calculate the direct conjugate concentration and amount of BSA/azide stabilizing solution to add. Add the stabilizing solution to the conjugation solution and mix thoroughly. Centrifuge briefly to remove liquid from the underside of the cap.

The Desired Concentration will be calculated on the D/P Calculation Sheet. Please note that the conjugates should be at a concentration < 300 μg/mL , adjust with calculated amount of fresh 1X PBS and stabilizing solution to reach appropriate concentration.
Transfer the stabilized DC to an amber tube and store at 2 °C - 8 °C .
Label the direct conjugate tubes with: biomarker name, dye, D/P, concentration, date of conjugation and vial #.
Input the conjugate into your reagent management system, if any.
Complete the Direct Conjugate Certificate of Analysis (see following section).

Antibody Information Target Vendor Catalog Lot Conjugate Information Controls Batch Record Verification: Conjugates are between Cy3 3.0 and 6.5 D/P: ⬜ Yes ⬜No (check one) Cy3 1.5 and 4.5 D/P: ⬜ Yes ⬜No (check one) Performance Verification: Conforms? ⬜ Yes ⬜No (check one) Representative Image(s) We confirm that this product has been verified and conforms to previously outlined QC measures Release Date: Verified By Name: Verified By Signature:
Open the NanoDrop 2000c software version 7.2.1.1 program located on the desktop of the NanonDrop computer.

Initially perform a pilot conjugation (~ 35 µg - 60 µg ) to determine the optimal D/P of the antibody. Then assume a 100% efficiency for the scale up conjugation, targeting the optimal D/P selected after the initial conjugation. This will avoid the possibility of over labeling the antibody. Please save 5 µg, purified Ab for any trouble shooting.
Antibody should be purified and in 1X PBS (w/azide).
If the antibody was ordered as purified, confirm there aren't any incompatible additives by checking the data sheet and the Purification Guide section of the Chemistry SOP.

Take a reading of the antibody using the NanoDrop (refer to section 'Quantitative Assessment of Pilot Direct Conjugate via NanoDrop2000c' of Pilot Conjugation) to check the concentration and ensure it is pure. If the concentration is much higher than what the vial says, then this indicates the presence of BSA, which will interfere with the conjugation. The antibody will need to be purified if this is the case. If antibody concentration is below 0.5 mg/mL , concentrate to 1 mg/mL using Amicon filters. Refer to Section 7.1 ('Evaluate Need for Purification as per Formulation') of the Cell DIVE Antibody Purification manual. Cell DIVE™ Platform | Antibody Purification Chemistry by Emily Hasser
Use purified antibody (35 μg minimum) at a concentration of ≤ 1.1 mg/mL .
Be sure to save at least 5 µg, unconjugated primary antibody to be validated alongside the direct conjugate.
Prepare the 1X dye solution and measure the concentration as described previously (Section 'Determining molar concentration of 1X Cy Dye via NanoDrop 2000c' of Pilot Conjugation).

Use the optimal D/P obtained after the pilot conjugation as a target. Assume a 100% efficiency upon scaleup. The “Loading” value used in Form-7 will be the optimal D/P. For example, if you determined the optimal D/P was 4.0, than the “Loading” parameter in Form-7 will be 4.0. This will prevent the possibility of overlabeling the antibody.

Measure the concentration of the antibody as described in Section 7.8 ('Confirming the complete removal of purified antibody via NanoDrop') in the Purification Manual. Cell DIVE™ Platform | Antibody Purification Chemistry by Emily Hasser

Prepare the antibody conjugation solution as described previously (Section 'Preparing calculations for pilot conjugations' of Pilot Conjugation). Use Form-7 to determine the appropriate amounts of all the reagents.
Add the appropriate amount of dye while vortexing at low speed.
Leave the mixture in complete darkness at Room temperature for 00:30:00 .

Prepare Zeba desalting columns (0.5 mL) immediately following the start of incubation. If you are conjugating more than 70 μg of antibody, then one Zeba column will not be enough. You can split the conjugation between two columns (4 columns total per conjugation). If you are conjugating more than 150 μg of antibody, switch to PD-10 columns.
PD-10 columns are gravity fed and will need a longer preparation time. The standard sample size is 1 mL - 2.5 mL . If a lower volume is used, the conjugate will be further diluted.
Remove the top cap and discard the column storage solution.
Cut the sealed end of the column at the notch.
Fill the column with 1X PBS and let the buffer enter the packed resin.
Repeat this filling and elution 4 times, then discard the flow-through.
Add 1 mL - 2.5 mL, conjugation solution to the top of the column. Dilute your conjugate with 1X PBS if volume is less than 1 mL .

Once the conjugate solution has completely entered the column, elute with 1X PBS until the colored conjugate has completely eluted. NOTE: If the color is hard to see, the eluent fractions can be measured with the NanoDrop to determine if all the conjugate has been eluted.
Measure D/P and determine yield and efficiency using procedure described earlier.
If the D/P calculated is too low, more dye can be conjugated using the established procedure.
Once an acceptable D/P ratio has been achieved, add stabilizing solution, 1X PBS and determine final conjugate antibody concentration.
Label and store vials at 4 °C .
Complete paperwork.
Submit material for revalidation vs original direct conjugate.
Remove the antibody from it's storage location and allow it to equilibrate to Room temperature for 00:45:00
creator: Liz McDonough
